original_abstract:
Abstract

The establishment of biosimilarity for monoclonal antibodies (mAbs) relies on a rigorous demonstration of comparability to an originator product through extensive analytical and functional testing. This study describes the physicochemical and biological characterization of GP2013, a proposed biosimilar to the chimeric anti-CD20 antibody rituximab.

An exhaustive suite of state-of-the-art techniques was employed to evaluate the primary and higher-order structures, post-translational modifications, and purity profiles of GP2013 in comparison to several batches of the originator product. Mass spectrometry-based sequencing and peptide mapping confirmed an identical primary amino acid sequence. Higher-order structural integrity was verified using multiple orthogonal methods, including X-ray crystallography, 1D $^1$H NMR, hydrogen-deuterium exchange mass spectrometry (HDX-MS), and differential scanning calorimetry, all of which showed GP2013 to be indistinguishable from the originator. 

Assessment of charge variants, glycosylation patterns, and size heterogeneity (including aggregates and sub-visible particles) demonstrated a high degree of similarity, with minor differences—such as C-terminal proline amidation—thoroughly evaluated and found to have no impact on safety or pharmacology. Functionally, GP2013 was compared to the originator using surface plasmon resonance (SPR) for Fc-receptor binding and a battery of cell-based bioassays, including target binding, antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity (ADCC), complement-dependent cytotoxicity (CDC), and apoptosis. In all cases, the potency and binding affinities of GP2013 were well within the ranges established for the originator product.

These findings demonstrate that GP2013 is highly similar to originator rituximab in both structure and function. This comprehensive analytical foundation supports the clinical development of GP2013 as a biosimilar, offering a more accessible therapeutic option for patients requiring rituximab therapy.
humanized_abstract:
Abstract

Determination of biosimilarity between a proposed biosimilar monoclonal antibody (mAb) and its originator mAb is reliant on the provision of substantial evidence of their mutual characteristics, derived from their thorough analysis and evaluation. Therefore, in this study, we have characterized GP2013, a proposed biosimilar to the chimeric anti-CD20 antibody rituximab.

An array of sophisticated analytical techniques was used to characterise the primary and secondary structure as well as the post translational modifications and the purity of GP2013 and to compare it with various batches of the reference product. Sequence and peptide mapping with MS confirmed an identical amino acid sequence. Integrity of the secondary structure of the product was confirmed by using an array of orthogonal analytical techniques such as X-ray crystallography, 1D $^1$H NMR, HDX-MS and DSC and these indicated that the product is same as that of the reference product.

Charge variant analysis, glycan analysis and size heterogeneity analysis including aggregates and sub-visible particles revealed a high degree of identity with minor differences being carefully evaluated and deemed to be non-relevant and not impacting safety or function. The function of the GP2013 monoclonal antibody was compared with the function of the Innovator antibody using surface plasmon resonance (SPR) to assess its binding to its Fc-receptors and a panel of cell-based bioassays assessing its target binding, antibody dependent cell mediated cytotoxicity (ADCC), complement dependent cytotoxicity (CDC) and apoptosis activity. These analyses confirmed that the potency and affinity of GP2013 for all of the tested receptors/cells were within the ranges of the values for the Innovator antibody.

Characterisation of a Next-Generation Rituximab Biosimilar Candidate, GP2013 This study confirms that GP2013 is highly similar to the innovator rituximab (MabThera) in terms of both its primary and secondary physical and biological characteristics. Collectively, the data provide a robust analytical scaffold upon which to pursue further clinical development of GP2013, potentially paving the way for the approval and launch of a meaningful rituximab biosimilar.
